commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=811502941a1030fe21729384ec9a9b9bd5227e75)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a generated-media proof marker to the mock OpenAI server, enables image generation in the Telegram proof SUT, documents the Mantis stimulus, and adds script-level tests.

PR surface: Tests +79, Docs +9, Other +34. Total +122 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main already directly recovers missing generated media in the generated_media_missing path, while the new mock marker returns a text completion rather than forcing the outer wake-false condition. I did not run the live Mantis lane in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer follow-up are needed because the remaining work is a valid proof-hook design plus real behavior evidence, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: [codex] Add generated media wake proof harness
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

This is a proof-harness follow-up for #97275, #95910, and #86034. The current Telegram Desktop Mantis harness cannot produce live proof for the internal generated-media wake-false path because the local Telegram SUT does not expose `image_generate`, and the mock OpenAI server does not drive an image-generation function call followed by the completion wake.

## Why This Change Was Made

#97275 is intentionally scoped to the production fix. This PR keeps the proof infrastructure separate so Mantis can later run the same generated-media scenario on baseline and candidate, with the expected visible delta in native Telegram Desktop.

## User Impact

No production media delivery behavior changes. This only changes the Mantis/Telegram proof harness and its mock model path:

- enables a mock image-generation model in the Telegram proof SUT
- allows the QA harness to call the loopback mock image endpoint
- adds a dedicated `OPENCLAW_E2E_GENERATED_MEDIA_WAKE_FALSE_PROOF` marker path in the mock OpenAI server
- documents the Mantis stimulus and baseline/candidate expectations
- adds tests covering the marker path and prompt/SUT constraints

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/e2e-mock-config-limits.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/e2e-mock-config-limits.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof-workflow.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/e2e-mock-config-limits.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof-workflow.test.ts .github/codex/prompts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof.md`
- `git diff --check`

Note: a broader oxlint invocation that included unmodified `test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts` surfaced an existing `unicorn(no-array-sort)` issue at line 289, so the passing oxlint check above is scoped to this PR's changed files.
target.author: tianxiaochannel-oss88
